# Nested anchor tag hydration error (2026-01-30 04:17Z)

_**User**_

In HTML, <a> cannot be a descendant of <a>.
This will cause a hydration error.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is getting a hydration error because there's a nested `<a>` tag inside another `<a>` tag. Looking at the error, it seems like:

1. There's a `LinkComponent` (which renders as `<a>`) wrapping a row
2. Inside that row, there's another `<a>` tag that links to a provider website (like "https://cartesia.ai")

This is happening in the STT page. Let me look at the STT page to understand the structure and fix this issue.</details></think>

Let me look at the STT page to understand the issue.

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **read_file_v2** • Read file: /Users/aman/Documents/repos/artpark/pense-frontend/src/app/stt/page.tsx</summary>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I can see the issue now. On line 270, there's a `<Link>` component (which renders as `<a>`) wrapping the entire row. Inside this Link, on line 286-307, there's another `<a>` tag for the provider website link.

This is invalid HTML - you can't nest `<a>` tags inside each other.

The fix is to change the inner `<a>` tag to something else, like a `<button>` that opens the link programmatically, or use a different approach. 

The best solution here would be to:
1. Change the inner `<a>` to a `<button>` that uses `window.open()` to open the link
2. Or change the parent `<Link>` to a `<div>` with an onClick handler

Since the row should be clickable to navigate to the job detail page, and the provider website links should also be clickable, I'll change the inner `<a>` to a `<button>` that opens the link using `window.open()`. This preserves the functionality while avoiding the nested anchor issue.</details></think>

---

I can see the issue. The page has a `<Link>` component (which renders as `<a>`) wrapping each job row (line 270), and inside it there's another `<a>` tag for the provider website (line 286). This creates a nested anchor which is invalid HTML.

The fix is to change the inner `<a>` tag to a `<button>` that opens the link programmatically.

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /Users/aman/Documents/repos/artpark/pense-frontend/src/app/stt/page.tsx</summary>

</details>

</tool-use>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The fix has been applied. I changed the inner `<a>` tag to a `<button>` that uses `window.open()` to open the provider website in a new tab. This preserves the same functionality while avoiding the nested anchor issue.

The key changes:
1. Changed `<a>` to `<button type="button">`
2. Removed `href`, `target`, and `rel` attributes
3. Added `e.preventDefault()` alongside `e.stopPropagation()` to prevent the parent Link from being triggered
4. Used `window.open(website, "_blank", "noopener,noreferrer")` to open the link in a new tab with the same security settings

This should fix the hydration error.</details></think>

---

The fix has been applied. I changed the inner `<a>` tag to a `<button>` element that uses `window.open()` to open the provider website in a new tab. This preserves the same functionality (clicking the icon opens the website, clicking elsewhere navigates to the job detail page) while avoiding the nested anchor issue that was causing the hydration error.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I found the "Gotchas & Edge Cases" section. Now I need to find the "UI Patterns" section within it and add the nested anchor gotcha there. Let me look at line 2484 onward which is the "UI Patterns" subsection. I'll add the nested anchor gotcha there since it's a UI-related issue.

The pattern I'll add is:
- When using `<Link>` from Next.js (which renders as `<a>`), you cannot nest other `<a>` tags inside. This causes hydration errors.
- Solution: Use `<button>` with `window.open()` for nested clickable elements within a Link.

Let me find a good place to add this within the UI Patterns section.</details></think>
